sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Why Is Fastenal (FAST) Up 3.1% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) shares have increased by 3.1% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500. The company reported Q4 2025 earnings and sales that met expectations, driven by strong contract customer momentum and improved operating leverage, with daily sales rising across manufacturing end markets and digital channels. Fastenal's management remains confident in long-term growth despite a challenging industrial environment.

feed.title: Vanda Stock Draws Heavy Buzz Ahead Of FDA Verdict On Bysanti For Bipolar, Schizophrenia
feed.summary: Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc. (VNDA) stock is generating significant retail investor interest as the FDA's decision approaches for Bysanti, its investigational drug for acute bipolar I disorder and schizophrenia. The FDA has set February 21 as the target decision date for the New Drug Application, which Vanda submitted in March 2025. If approved, Bysanti could launch in the U.S. this year, and Vanda is also exploring its use for major depressive disorder.

feed.title: Fiverr International Ltd. (FVRR) PT Lowered to $16 at RBC Capital
feed.summary: RBC Capital analyst Brad Erickson lowered the price target for Fiverr International Ltd. (FVRR) to $16 from $28, while maintaining a Sector Perform rating. The downgrade follows a mixed Q4 report and a 2026 outlook that suggests AI headwinds are significantly impacting the company's fundamentals, particularly in simpler service categories. Despite some diversification efforts towards larger customers, the analyst believes a full stabilization of the stock's multiple requires clear evidence of more complex categories offsetting AI disruptions.

feed.title: BlueScope Gets $11B 'Final' Bid From SGH, Steel Dynamics
feed.summary: Australia's BlueScope Steel Ltd. is reviewing an unsolicited, revised buyout bid worth $11 billion from SGH Ltd. and Steel Dynamics Inc. The bid was announced on Wednesday and is considered a "final" offer by the bidders.

feed.title: Compugen to Participate in Upcoming Investor Conferences
feed.summary: Compugen Ltd. announced its participation in two upcoming investor conferences: the Oppenheimer 36th Annual Healthcare Life Sciences Conference on February 26, 2026, and the Leerink Partners 2026 Global Healthcare Conference on March 9, 2026. Management will hold fireside chats and 1x1 meetings at both virtual and in-person events. Live webcasts of the fireside chats will be available on Compugen's investor relations website, with replays accessible afterward.

feed.title: Why NeoGenomics (NEO) Shares Are Falling Today
feed.summary: Shares of NeoGenomics (NEO) fell over 10% after the oncology diagnostics company reported fourth-quarter results that beat expectations but issued a full-year 2026 forecast falling short of analyst predictions. Despite an 11% revenue growth in Q4 2025, the increased net loss for the full year 2025 and lower-than-anticipated 2026 guidance led to a negative market reaction. The company's stock has been highly volatile, with significant drops in the past year, and is currently trading 22.6% below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Roper Technologies announces $0.91 per share dividend for April By Investing.com
feed.summary: Roper Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:ROP) has declared a dividend of $0.91 per share, payable on April 22, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 6, 2026. This marks the company's 12th consecutive year of dividend increases and 35th year of dividend maintenance, with a current yield of 1.11%. The announcement follows their Q4 2025 earnings, where EPS beat estimates but revenue slightly missed.

feed.title: Corporacion Inmobiliaria Vesta (NYSE:VTMX) Issues Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $1.21 EPS
feed.summary: Corporacion Inmobiliaria Vesta (NYSE:VTMX) exceeded analyst expectations for its quarterly earnings, reporting an EPS of $2.01 against an estimated $0.80, though revenue of $76.4 million fell short of the $1.29 billion consensus. The company also announced an increased annual dividend of $0.2041 per share, reflecting a 63.0% yield, but noted an unsustainable payout ratio of 850%. Despite the earnings beat, analyst ratings are mixed, with a "Reduce" consensus and a target price of $33.33, while the stock traded flat at $33.62.

feed.title: State OKs rate hike for DTE Electric payers after reliability improves
feed.summary: The Michigan Public Service Commission approved a 4.6% rate hike for DTE Electric customers, effective March 5, to fund continued distribution system upgrades and tree trimming. The increase, which will add $4.93 to a typical residential customer's monthly bill, aims to build on improved reliability. The $242 million revenue gain from the rate hike is significantly less than the $574 million DTE initially requested.

feed.title: DTE wins approval of $242 million annual electric rate increase
feed.summary: The Michigan Public Service Commission approved a $242 million annual electric rate increase for DTE, significantly less than the company's initial request of $574 million. Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel opposed the hike, noting that DTE has received $1 billion in annual rate increases since 2020 and has another request pending for natural gas customers, while Consumers Energy also has pending rate hike requests. The increase affects 2.3 million DTE customers in Southeast Michigan and the Thumb region.

feed.title: Insights into Live Nation Entertainment Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Live Nation Entertainment (NYSE: LYV) reported its Q4 earnings, beating estimated EPS by 7.69% with $-0.96 against an estimate of $-1.04. Revenue increased by $632.00 million year-over-year. Despite the earnings beat this quarter, the company's share price saw a 10.59% drop the day after last quarter due to missing EPS.

feed.title: Walgreens deal veteran Lanesha Minnix to lead BD legal and public affairs
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has appointed Lanesha Minnix as Executive Vice President and General Counsel, effective March 16, 2026. Minnix, who previously held senior legal roles at Walgreens Boots Alliance where she played a key part in a $24 billion take-private transaction, will lead BD's Law Group and Public Affairs and join the Executive Leadership Team. This appointment comes as BD navigates significant strategic changes, including portfolio adjustments and debt management.

feed.title: Vistra to pay $0.228 per share, $40 on preferred in March–April
feed.summary: Vistra (NYSE: VST) has declared a quarterly common-stock dividend of $0.2280 per share, amounting to an estimated aggregate payment of approximately $75 million for the quarter. The common dividend is payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 20, 2026. Additionally, the company announced a semi-annual dividend of $40.00 per preferred share on its 8.0% Series A preferred stock, payable April 15, 2026, to holders of record as of April 1, 2026.

feed.title: Select Water Solutions Announces Public Offering of Common Stock
feed.summary: Select Water Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: WTTR) announced the commencement of an underwritten public offering of $175.0 million of its Class A common stock. The company plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including water infrastructure growth, potential acquisitions, or debt repayment. J.P. Morgan Securities LLC and BofA Securities are serving as lead book-running managers for the offering.

feed.title: Select Water seeks $175M to fund water projects, acquisitions, cut debt
feed.summary: Select Water Solutions (NYSE: WTTR) announced a proposed underwritten public offering of $175.0 million of Class A common stock, with a 30-day underwriter option for additional shares. The company plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including water infrastructure growth capital projects, potential acquisitions, or debt repayment under its sustainability-linked credit facility. Following the news, WTTR's stock declined 6.61%, reflecting market concerns about dilution despite previous operational momentum.

feed.title: West Coast apartment REIT lifts dividend for 32nd year in a row
feed.summary: Essex Property Trust, a West Coast apartment REIT (NYSE: ESS), has announced its 32nd consecutive annual dividend increase of 0.8%. The first quarter dividend is set at $2.59 per share, totaling $10.36 annually. The company also announced its annual shareholders' meeting for May 12, 2026.

feed.title: Corporación Inmobiliaria Vesta Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Results
feed.summary: Corporación Inmobiliaria Vesta announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, with rental revenues exceeding guidance and significant increases in Adjusted NOI and Adjusted EBITDA. The company also highlighted strong leasing activity, improved occupancy rates, and significant progress on sustainability targets, while providing positive guidance for 2026.

feed.title: Pool Corporation Q4 2025 Earnings Call Insights
feed.summary: Pool Corporation reported stable revenue of $5.3 billion for 2025 and Q4 sales of $982 million, despite a slowdown in new pool construction, demonstrating a solid market position. The company improved its gross margin to 29.7% for 2025 due to cost control and operational efficiency, and distributed $530 million to shareholders, including a 4% dividend increase. Management projects low single-digit net sales growth for 2026, supported by resilient maintenance revenues and digital expansion.

feed.title: APPLIED MATERIALS INC /DE SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Applied Materials Inc. has released its Q3 Form 10-Q report, detailing financial and operational performance. The company reported a slight revenue decrease due to reduced demand in logic systems but saw an increase in net income and diluted EPS driven by equity investments. Strategic moves include shifting the 200mm equipment business to the Semiconductor Systems segment and focusing on product development and service growth to maintain a competitive edge.

feed.title: Pepsi Ducks Class Action Claims in Price Discrimination Case
feed.summary: A federal judge dismissed class action allegations against PepsiCo Inc. and its Frito Lay North America Inc. unit, which were accused of price discrimination against independent convenience stores. The ruling significantly reduces PepsiCo's financial exposure, as the case can no longer proceed with thousands of potential plaintiffs. Only two plaintiffs, Alqosh Enterprises Inc. and NMRM Inc., remain in the lawsuit.

feed.title: Pentair Stock After Earnings: Quiet Winner Hiding in Plain Sight?
feed.summary: Pentair plc (PNR) recently reported solid earnings, raising guidance and reinforcing its position as a quiet winner in the industrial sector, particularly within water infrastructure, pool equipment, and water solutions. The company's disciplined operations and focus on higher-margin products have allowed it to maintain resilient margins despite a challenging macro environment. For US investors, Pentair offers a blend of growth, income, and secular water exposure, making it an attractive consideration for diversified portfolios.

feed.title: 12 Information Technology Stocks Moving In Thursday's After-Market Session
feed.summary: This article details the performance of twelve Information Technology stocks during Thursday's after-market session, highlighting significant gainers and losers. Gainers include Wetouch Technology, CISO Global, Cloudastructure, CXApp, Xerox Holdings, and Ichor Holdings. Conversely, Akamai Technologies, Iveda Solutions, Black Titan, Atomera, Urgently, and Rackspace Technology experienced declines, with Akamai's drop attributed to its Q4 earnings report.

feed.title: Data center giant Digital Realty to pay $1.22 a share on Mar. 31
feed.summary: Digital Realty (NYSE: DLR) announced quarterly cash dividends for both common and preferred stock for Q1 2026. The company authorized a $1.22 per-share common dividend, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 13, 2026. Additionally, dividends for preferred Series J, K, and L were declared at $0.328125, $0.365625, and $0.325000 per share, respectively, with the same record and payment dates.

feed.title: Weave Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Weave Communications, Inc. announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, with revenue up 17.0% year over year for both periods, reaching $63.4 million and $239.0 million, respectively. The company also reported record gross margins and operating income, along with significant growth in net cash provided by operating activities and free cash flow. Weave provided a positive outlook for the first quarter and full year 2026, anticipating continued growth in revenue and non-GAAP income from operations.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton Canada Announces Investment Fund Updates, including Fee Reductions and Series Terminations
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton Canada has announced several updates to its investment funds, including fee reductions for the Franklin Innovation Fund effective March 1, 2026. The company also proposed investment objective changes for three balanced funds, subject to investor approval in May 2026, to allow for greater flexibility in asset allocation or different investment focuses. Additionally, Franklin Templeton Canada will terminate certain High Net Worth Series of mutual funds and delist and terminate ETF series of the Franklin ClearBridge International Growth Fund and Franklin Global Growth Fund by May 15, 2026.

feed.title: Synopsys Appoints Former Deloitte Executive Peter Shimer to Board of Directors and Announces Board Transitions
feed.summary: Synopsys, Inc. has appointed Peter A. Shimer, a former Deloitte executive with extensive experience in finance and corporate governance, to its board of directors, effective immediately. Shimer will serve on the board's audit committee, bringing valuable expertise in advising on complex business matters. The company also announced that Luis Borgen and Dr. Ajei Gopal will not be renominated for re-election, reflecting the board's commitment to ongoing refreshment and evolution, especially as the Ansys integration progresses.

feed.title: Evertec's 5-cent dividend set, March 6 payout to holders
feed.summary: EVERTEC, Inc. (NYSE: EVTC) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.05 per share, to be paid on March 6, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 2, 2026. The Board of Directors declared this dividend on February 19, 2026, and anticipates declaring it regularly in future quarters, subject to approval and potential adjustments. EVERTEC is a leading full-service transaction processor and financial technology provider in Latin America, Puerto Rico, and the Caribbean.

feed.title: GitLab Chief Executive Officer Bill Staples and GitLab Chief Financial Officer Jessica Ross to Present at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference
feed.summary: GitLab announced that CEO Bill Staples and CFO Jessica Ross will present at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 5, 2026, at 9:15 a.m. Pacific Time. The fireside chat will be webcast live and a replay will be available on GitLab's investor relations website. GitLab is recognized as an intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps, trusted by over 50 million users and 50% of Fortune 100 companies.

feed.title: Akamai Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies, Inc. reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year ended December 31, 2025, with better-than-expected performance in both top and bottom lines. The company saw significant growth in its Cloud Infrastructure Services and Guardicore Segmentation and API Security products. Akamai also provided financial guidance for the first quarter and full year 2026, anticipating continued growth in revenue and non-GAAP net income per diluted share.

feed.title: Expert Outlook: Archer-Daniels-Midland Through The Eyes Of 4 Analysts
feed.summary: Four analysts have provided ratings for Archer-Daniels-Midland (NYSE: ADM), with sentiments ranging from indifferent to somewhat bearish. Their 12-month price targets average $59.75, an increase of 0.84% from the previous average. The company shows strong financial performance with a market capitalization above industry average, impressive net margin, high ROE, and effective debt management, despite a recent revenue decline.

feed.title: Shareholders that lost money on PayPal Holdings, Inc.(PYPL)
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP is notifying investors in PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) about a class action lawsuit for alleged securities fraud between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit claims PayPal made misleading statements about its financial targets and growth potential for its Branded Checkout segment, despite the salesforce not being equipped to execute said growth. This led to a significant stock price decline on February 3, 2026, after disappointing Q4 2025 results and withdrawn 2027 financial targets.

feed.title: IT ACTIVE INVESTIGATION: Contact Levi & Korsinsky if you lost money on your Gartner, Inc. investment
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky has initiated an investigation into Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) for potential violations of federal securities laws, specifically regarding their financial disclosures and non-GAAP measures. The probe focuses on Gartner's Q4 earnings report, which highlighted an EPS beat but understated revenue shortfalls and a declining 2026 outlook, as well as the realism of previous EPS guidance and share-count assumptions. This investigation comes after Gartner's shares dropped over 20% following the earnings release, reaching a new 52-week low.

feed.title: Citigroup Cuts Empire State Realty Trust (NYSE:ESRT) Price Target to $7.00
feed.summary: Citigroup has lowered its price target for Empire State Realty Trust (NYSE:ESRT) from $8.00 to $7.00, while maintaining a "neutral" rating. This adjustment reflects a cautious analyst sentiment, with other firms like Wells Fargo also reducing their targets and the stock trading near a 12-month low of $6.02. Despite these cuts, the consensus rating from MarketBeat for ESRT remains "Hold" with an average target price of $7.70.

feed.title: Targa Resources Corp. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Targa Resources Corp. has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing significant financial and operational growth, including total revenues of over $17 billion and a 24% increase in operating income. The company expanded its processing capacity and NGL pipeline infrastructure and is focused on continued growth through strategic expansions, acquisitions, and capital management. Targa also outlined various market, operational, regulatory, and cybersecurity risks, along with challenges from inflation and monetary policy.

feed.title: Global X Copper Miners ETF (4COP.F) Watch: After-Hours Volume Hits 6,483 at €29.30
feed.summary: The Global X Copper Miners ETF (4COP.F) saw significant after-hours trading, with 6,483 shares at €29.30, indicating strong investor interest in copper. This ETF offers diversified exposure to major copper mining companies, benefiting from global industrial demand, supply constraints, inflation hedging, and the green energy transition. Investors can use 4COP.F for short-term trading, long-term exposure to commodity growth, and portfolio risk hedging, though they should be mindful of copper price volatility and operational risks.

feed.title: KeyBank hires PNC exec to drive merchant services sales in Pittsburgh, Cleveland
feed.summary: KeyBank has hired Josh Kopp, a former PNC territory manager, to serve as its new merchant services sales leader for the Pittsburgh and Cleveland areas. Kopp will focus on expanding payment solutions for businesses in these regions. This move is part of KeyBank's strategy to bolster its merchant services offerings.

feed.title: DFW deals roundup: Caterpillar completes buyout, fintech raises $25 million
feed.summary: DFW has seen recent merger and acquisition activity, including Caterpillar completing a buyout. Additionally, a McKinney-based fintech company called Uptiq successfully raised $25 million in a Series B funding round.

feed.title: Insider Buying: Joseph Nerges Acquires Additional Shares of CSP Inc (CSPI)
feed.summary: Joseph Nerges, a 10% owner of CSP Inc (CSPI), recently purchased an additional 11,012 shares on February 19, 2026, increasing his total holdings to 1,406,075 shares. This purchase is part of a larger trend of insider buying at CSPI, with seven insider buys and no sells in the past year. The stock is currently trading at $9.22, suggesting it is modestly undervalued compared to its GF Value of $11.52.

feed.title: MKSI: Citigroup Raises Price Target to $295 | MKSI Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for MKS Instruments, Inc. (MKSI) to $295 from $185, maintaining a "Buy" rating. This significant adjustment reflects continued analyst optimism, with other firms also increasing their price targets for MKSI. The company, known for its instruments and process control systems, primarily sees revenue from its Vacuum Solutions segment.

feed.title: Chevron names new leaders for supply and trading, production and strategy
feed.summary: Chevron has announced a series of senior leadership changes effective throughout 2026, impacting its supply and trading, domestic shale production, and corporate strategy departments. These changes include key retirements and new appointments, with the company continuing its integration of Hess following its acquisition in July 2025. The reshuffle aims to strengthen U.S. fuel operations and optimize strategy and production efforts.

feed.title: CISS C3is Inc. (NASDAQ) rises 47.37% to $2.24 on Q4 report 19 Feb 2026: Key outlook
feed.summary: C3is Inc. (CISS) stock surged by 47.37% to $2.24 following its Q4 2025 earnings report and the announcement of a 1-for-20 reverse stock split. The company highlighted steady cash flow from handysize time charters and exposure from an Aframax tanker. Meyka AI rates CISS with a "HOLD" suggestion and projects a 1-year target of $8.85, indicating a potential upside of 295.09%, while noting risks associated with liquidity and the reverse split.

feed.title: Black Hawk Acquisition Announces Sponsor-Backed Convertible Note Financing
feed.summary: Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation (BKHA) has announced a sponsor-backed convertible note financing of up to $300,000 from Black Hawk Management LLC to cover working capital needs. The note bears 10% annual interest and matures upon the initial business combination or liquidation, with the sponsor having the option to receive cash repayment or convert the note into equity at $1.00 per share in a DeSPAC transaction. This flexible financing structure supports the SPAC's deal execution while aligning the sponsor's incentives with the combined company's future performance.

feed.title: Engine Trouble? GE Aerospace Rises While RTX Falls.
feed.summary: Airbus's unexpected financial guidance for 2026 is having a significant impact on the aerospace supply chain, particularly for engine manufacturers. This has led to a divergence in performance between GE Aerospace, which is rising, and RTX, which is experiencing a decline. The article highlights how Airbus's guidance extends beyond its competition with Boeing and affects companies like RTX and GE Aerospace.

feed.title: Argan, Inc. Adjusts Valuation Metrics Amid Strong Financial Fundamentals and Bullish Trends
feed.summary: Argan, Inc. has recently seen a valuation adjustment from expensive to fair, with strong financial fundamentals including a low debt-to-equity ratio and robust cash position. Technical indicators now suggest a bullish trend, supported by positive MACD and Bollinger Bands signals. The company, a small-cap construction player, boasts a P/E ratio of 33, a PEG ratio of 0.22, and significant institutional ownership.

feed.title: ARRAY Technologies Upsizes and Extends Revolving Credit Facility to $370 Million, Strengthening Liquidity and Strategic Flexibility
feed.summary: ARRAY Technologies (NASDAQ: ARRY) has successfully upsized and extended its revolving credit facility from $166 million to $370 million, pushing its maturity to February 18, 2031. This move significantly enhances the company's liquidity, supports operational execution, and provides flexibility for global growth initiatives. The expanded facility, which includes several major banking partners, reflects strong confidence in ARRAY's strategy and its position as a global leader in utility-scale solar technology.

feed.title: (WABF) as a Liquidity Pulse for Institutional Tactics
feed.summary: This article provides an AI-driven analysis of Western Asset Bond Etf (NASDAQ: WABF), highlighting a neutral sentiment across all time horizons. It outlines three institutional trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—with specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels. The analysis emphasizes current signals, support, and resistance levels derived from multi-timeframe signal analysis.

feed.title: C3is reports sharp rise in 2025 net income
feed.summary: Greek shipowner C3is reported a significant increase in net income for 2025, reaching $10.5 million, a 481% rise from the previous year. The company's EBITDA also grew by 244% to $17 million, driven by fleet expansion and operational efficiency. C3is ended the year with $14.9 million in cash and is financing two new product tanker acquisitions with an interest-free loan.

feed.title: Avis Budget Narrows Q4 Loss as Adjusted EBITDA Turns Positive
feed.summary: Avis Budget Group (NASDAQ: CAR) reported a narrower Q4 2025 net loss and a positive Adjusted EBITDA, despite a 2% decline in consolidated revenue. The company’s Americas segment saw a revenue decrease but improved Adjusted EBITDA, while the International segment experienced increased profitability. For fiscal year 2025, Avis Budget Group also reduced its net loss and increased full-year Adjusted EBITDA, projecting further growth in 2026.

feed.title: Roper Technologies Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.91 a Share, Payable April 22 to Holders of Record April 6
feed.summary: Roper Technologies has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.91 per share. The dividend is scheduled for payment on April 22 to shareholders of record as of April 6. This decision reflects the company's continued commitment to returning value to its investors.

feed.title: MARTIN MARIETTA MATERIALS INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Martin Marietta Materials Inc. has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, revealing strong financial performance with $6,150 million in revenues and a focus on aggregates-led growth and strategic acquisitions. The company detailed its financial highlights, business initiatives, and an outlook anticipating continued strong demand in nonresidential construction for 2026. However, the report also outlined various challenges and risks, including market cyclicality, operational sensitivities to weather, and regulatory changes, which the company aims to mitigate through strategic planning and investments.
